title: Voice assistant-enabled web application or web page
abstract: Various embodiments discussed herein enable applications to seamlessly contribute to executing voice commands of users via voice assistant functionality. In response to receiving a user request to open an application or web page, the application can request and responsively receive a voice assistant runtime component along with the application or web page. The application, using a particular universal application interface component can compile or interpret the voice assistant runtime component from a source code format to an intermediate code format. In response to the application or web page being rendered and the detection of a key word or phrase, the application can activate voice assistant command execution functionality. The user can issue a voice command after which the application along with specific services can help execute the voice command.
claims:
Claims 1. A computer-implemented method comprising: receiving, by a web browser operating on a user device associated with a user, a request to access a web page or web application; rendering, by the web browser, the web page or web application in response to the receiving of the request to access the web page or web application; receiving, by the web browser and not by the web page or the web application, an indication of a voice command; and based on the receiving of the voice command by the web browser, causing, at least in part and by the web browser, the voice command to be executed. 2. The method of claim 1, wherein the causing, at least in part the voice command to be executed includes using a WebAssembly protocol to encode the voice command and transmit the encoded voice command over a network for further processing. 3. The method of claim 1, wherein the causing, at least in part the voice command to be executed does not require an installation or download of a particular application to the user device. 4. The method of claim 1, further comprising enabling the voice command to be encoded into any standard of a plurality of standards for encoding the voice command. 5. The method of claim 1, further comprising causing synchronization between the web application and a voice assistant device different than the user device, wherein the synchronizing enables the a same set of voice commands to be executed from the voice assistant device and the user device based on the voice assistant device and the user device having a same set of capabilities for the execution of the same set of voice commands. 6. The method of claim 1, further comprising changing an application page of the web application based on the executing of the voice command. 7. The method of claim 1, wherein the causing, at least in part the voice command to be executed does not require a specific operating system, device, or browser. 8. One or more computer storage media having computer-executable instructions embodied thereon that, when executed, by one or more processors, cause the one or more processors to perform a method, the method comprising: 

 receiving, by a client application, a request from a user device to access a web application or a web page, the user device is associated with a user; causing, by the client application, the web application or the web page to be rendered in response to the receiving of the request from a user device to access the web application or the web page; subsequent to the rendering, detecting, by the client application, a voice utterance from the user; in response to the detecting of the voice utterance, triggering a component that is configured to record a given voice command from the user; receiving a voice command from the user and based at least in part on the triggering of the component, encoding the voice command of the user; and based at least in part on the encoding of the voice command, causing, at least in part and by the client application, the voice command to be executed. 9. The computer storage media of claim 8, wherein the causing, at least in part the voice command to be executed includes using a WebAssembly protocol to transmit the encoded voice command over a network to a natural language process service that interprets the voice command and one or more other services. 10. The computer storage media of claim 8, wherein the causing, at least in part the voice command to be executed does not require an installation or download of a particular application to the user device. 11. The computer storage media of claim 8, the method further comprising enabling the voice command to be encoded into any standard of a plurality of standards for encoding the voice command. 12. The computer storage media of claim 8, the method further comprising causing synchronization between the web page and a smart speaker device that is different than the user device, wherein the synchronizing enables the a same set of voice commands to be executed from the smart speaker device and the user device based on the smart speaker device and the user device having a same set of capabilities for the execution of the same set of voice commands. 13. The computer storage media of claim 8, the method further comprising causing the web application to change functionality based on the executing of the voice command. 14. The computer storage media of claim 8, wherein the causing, at least in part the voice command to be executed does not require a specific operating 

 system, device, or browser. 15. A system for implementing classification-based adjustable seek energy settings in storage device systems, the system comprising: one or more processors; and one or more computer storage media storing computer-useable instructions that, when used by the one or more processors, cause the one or more processors to perform a method, the method comprising: receiving a request from a user device to access an application or a web page, the user device is associated with a user; receiving a voice assistant runtime component associated with the application or web page, the voice assistant runtime component includes particular functionality to execute a voice command; compiling or interpreting the voice assistant runtime component from a source code format to an intermediate code format that is binary; in response to the compiling or interpreting of the voice assistant runtime component from the source code format to the intermediate code format, causing the application or web page to read the binary intermediate code; and based at least in part on the causing of the application or web page to read the binary intermediate code, causing, at least in part, a voice command of a user to be executed.
